PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-04-2024 Continued To Apr-29-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Kelly Dixon, Esq., for and with Marsha Maloof, Petitioner.
Zurvohn Maloof, in propria persona, Objector.

At the outset, the court confirms with Mr. Maloof that he objects to the instant petition and offers advocacy for his position stating that Ms. Dixon recuse herself. Ms. Dixon offers advocacy for the court's consideration to appoint her client as Personal Representative of the Estate and requests that the court overrule Mr. Maloof's oral objection. The court offers its position and advises that it seems appropriate for the parties to discuss and agree to the appointment of a third-party neutral to serve as Administrator. Ms. Dixon agrees with the court's suggestion and advises that she is amenable to meet and confer with Mr. Maloof regarding the preceding and possibly file a petition to appoint a fiduciary. Mr. Maloof appears to also agree with the court's suggestion.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to Apr-29-2024 at 9:00 a.m. If the parties are not able to agree to a third-party neutral, Mr. Maloof is instructed to file a written objection on or before Apr-03-2024.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-05-2024 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-04-2023 Continued To Feb-05-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Kelly Dixon, Esq., in court, for and with Marsha Maloof, proposed Executor.
Zurvohn Maloof, via Zoom, in propria persona, Objector.

The court begins by questioning the validity of the signature on the purported original will. Ms. Dixon and her client offer testimony regarding the court's concerns and in advocacy for the validity of the will. Mr. Maloof offers testimony to advocate for his position that the signature on the alleged will is not an original signature.

After additional discussion, including whether Ms. Dixon intends to request an evidentiary hearing, the subject matter is continued to Feb-05-2024 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Pliego/Singh. Not Reported.
